note: Researched 12 July 2026. Every count below was verified against the source linked on it that day. Counts that could not be verified say not counted rather than a guess.

synthesis.summary: The free-resource giants (Physics & Maths Tutor, Save My Exams, Seneca) own search traffic and notes, and the live-class space is contested by MyEdSpace, whose teacher-fronted TikTok accounts dwarf their brand account. Up Learn owns the money-back guarantee angle. Nobody in this list owns a diagnose-first method taught by a doctor, nobody is serious on LinkedIn for students, and nobody speaks to parents on Facebook with any care.
moves: Stay loud on LinkedIn. None of these seven is active there for students. It is genuinely uncontested and it is already your channel.
Treat results day like MyEdSpace treats it, but in your voice: the Rescue tool plus calm doctor-led posts, pre-written well before 13 August, helping rather than selling that morning.

Person-fronted video beats brand-fronted video in this market. MyEdSpace's teacher account has roughly nine times the brand account's followers. Your equivalent is Waleed-fronted diagnosis clips, one per bottleneck.
Do not compete on notes or past papers against PMT and Save My Exams. Compete on method and accountability, which the resource giants do not sell.
Tailored Tutors shows a personal brand alone does not scale (864 followers). Your diagnostic funnel is the difference. Point every piece of content at it.
watchNext: MyEdSpace's results day campaign in August
Up Learn's guarantee messaging around results week
Whether Save My Exams moves into live teaching

watchlist.name: MyEdSpace
watchlist.positioning: Live online classes at scale with teacher-fronted social accounts. Press coverage (Primary Times, Aug 2025) claims their teachers have over 4 million combined followers. The closest model to yours and the most aggressive marketer on results day.

watchlist.name: Up Learn
watchlist.positioning: VC-backed A-level courses selling an A star or A guarantee with your money back, claiming use by over 100,000 students. Premium price, heavy on proof and outcome claims.

watchlist.name: Physics & Maths Tutor
watchlist.positioning: The default free revision resource site for A-level students, with a paid tuition arm bolted on. Owns enormous organic search traffic; social presence is thin and fragmented.

watchlist.name: Save My Exams
watchlist.positioning: Subscription revision notes covering every board and subject, written by examiners. Competes for the same searches your blog targets.

watchlist.name: SnapRevise
watchlist.positioning: A-level and GCSE video courses. Pre-recorded rather than live, so weaker on accountability, which is your strength.

watchlist.name: Seneca Learning
watchlist.positioning: Free interactive learning platform with a premium tier. Huge user base through schools, shallow on method.

watchlist.name: Tailored Tutors
watchlist.positioning: A-level video courses and tutoring, strongest in Biology and Chemistry, fronted by a personal brand like yours but without a diagnostic funnel behind it.
